It is my hope that you are participating in the activities of anash and their farbrengens, and participating as is fitting. This means not only with your physical presence, but also by influencing your acquaintances and friends to do the same. This is the very essence of "spreading the wellsprings outward"—that even those who for the time being are on the outside, and have no desire to enter within, should have the wellsprings brought to them. For then, eventually, the desire will be awakened in them to enjoy these wellsprings in their fullness, and to enter within, and to the innermost chamber. The magnitude of this matter and the magnitude of the reward for one such as yourself require no elaboration. May it be His will that you do this with joy and peace of mind, and may you share good news regarding yourself and your wife, may she live.

With blessings,

**On behalf of the Rebbe, shlita**

**Secretary**

## 1882

16 Adar Sheni, 5717
Brooklyn

To the esteemed and distinguished individual, who engages in communal needs and accomplishes much,
R’ Shlomo, may he live,

Peace and blessing!

I confirm receipt of your letter in due time, and I have also now received the news regarding the success of the Purim performance and the warm part you took in it. May Hashem Yisbarach help you to be able to fulfill the directive of "ascending in holiness," in good health for you and your household, with peace of mind and ample livelihood. May we all, as one, merit to strengthen and expand the holy institutions of Chabad-Lubavitch in all places where they exist, in accordance with the demands of the time and place—demands which are growing ever greater. Hashem Yisbarach gives the ability to act in this regard from His full, open, holy, and generous hand; it depends only on the will of the person, not to be deterred by obstacles and hindrances—especially since, for the most part, they are nothing more than imagined, not real—and to perform actions with a whole and joyful heart, for then the success is even greater.

Coming now from the days of Purim, these days also provide the instruction that obstacles, hindrances, concealments, and obfuscations—in the final analysis—not only do not hinder, but they also become helpful, as the holy Torah says regarding the month of Purim: "And the month—that is, all the days of the month—which **was transformed** for them, etc., into joy, etc., into a holiday."

The meaning of "was transformed" is that even those matters which are not good in a visible and revealed way, this month has the capacity to transform them into joy and a holiday, visible even to the physical eyes.

With blessings for good tidings,

P.S.
The receipt of the funds is confirmed, and thank you.

## 583

B"H, 16 Adar Rishon, 5717
Brooklyn.

To the avreich Naftali, may he live,

Greetings and blessings!

It has been some time since we have received any news from you. It is my hope that you are continuing your participation in the Torah shiurim that are being studied, including the shiurim in Chassidus. Since Hashem has graced you with the talent to influence others through your writing, and your words are being printed—meaning they reach a wide circle of our fellow Jews—surely you are utilizing this channel as well for the aforementioned purpose, in the well-known terminology: spreading the wellsprings of Chassidus until they reach the outermost reaches.

The magnitude of the merit of this activity cannot be fully described, and one can see tangibly that it also brings great spiritual satisfaction. It would be pleasing for me to hear from you that you are increasing your efforts in these activities, both regarding others and regarding yourself, in accordance with the instruction of the prophet: that even when one shares his bread with the hungry, brings the poor into his home, and covers the naked, "you shall not hide yourself from your own flesh."

I trust you are participating in the farbrengens of anash in general, and in the farbrengens in Kfar Chabad in particular.

With blessings for good news regarding all the above.

***

**Naftali, may he live:** Kraus, Bnei Brak.

## 5,284

B"H, 17 Adar Sheni, 5717
Brooklyn, N.Y.

To the esteemed Rabbi, the G-d-fearing, Torah-learned, and involved in communal needs, etc.,
R' Eliezer Dovid, may he live and be well,

Greetings and blessings!

In response to your notification regarding the wedding of your daughter, Henya, may she live, with the bridegroom, the avreich, Rabbi Yosef, may he live.

I hereby express my blessing of mazal tov, mazal tov, and may it be in a good and auspicious hour. May they build a home in Israel, an everlasting edifice founded upon the Torah and the mitzvos as they are illuminated by the "light of Torah"—which is the Torah of Chassidus—and may he and his wife, may she live, derive from them an abundance of Chassidic nachas.

With blessings of mazal tov, mazal tov.

Regarding what you wrote to resolve the *tzarich iyun gadol* of R' Akiva Eiger on the Ran in Nedarim 79a, based on the reasoning of the Rashba in Kiddushin 50a:

It is *tzarich iyun gadol* how one can say this is the opinion of the Ran, seeing as he disagrees with the Rashba in the aforementioned place. See the Ran in Kiddushin there.

***

**R' Eliezer Dovid:** Brand, Jerusalem.

**Rabbi Yosef:** Aker.

**To resolve the *tzarich iyun gadol* of R' Akiva Eiger:** Regarding the words of the Ran, s.v. *Heifer belibo* [he nullified it in his heart], etc., "And it appears to me that we derive this from the silence of the day he heard, etc., and from this [we learn] that whenever he confirmed it in his heart, it is confirmed immediately." And in the *Gilyon HaShas*: "It is *tzarich iyun*—we hold that even though 'matters of the heart are not matters,' nevertheless, where it is proven and revealed to all, it is considered a matter, etc. If so, what is the proof from the fact that he remained silent the entire day of hearing, etc.? But if he confirmed it in his heart, which is not revealed to all, it is like all 'matters of the heart' which are ineffective, and it is *tzarich iyun*." And in my humble opinion, regarding the gravity of the question, one can say [it is resolved] based on the words of the Rashba in Kiddushin, page 50, etc.

## 585

585

B"H, 17 Adar Sheni, 5717
Brooklyn.

To the Committee of Kfar Chabad,
May Hashem protect them and grant them life.

Peace and blessing!

I have been informed that the vast majority of Gemach funds in Eretz Yisrael (may it be rebuilt and established) could potentially receive support. It seems the intention is that they would be granted a sum as a loan fund from certain offices in Eretz Yisrael, such as the Ministry of Welfare and similar bodies. Apparently, the conditions they impose are lenient, and their content is simply to ensure that the Gemach is managed with integrity and the like. It is surprising that, apparently, this is not yet known to you. In any event, it is necessary that you inquire into the details of these matters as soon as possible, as it is possible that this could significantly increase their capacity to provide loans.

Furthermore, according to reports, there is no contradiction if several Chabad Gemach funds submit this request, provided they are in different towns.

When Mr. Moshe Yaffe (from Manchester, of anash lineage), who now resides in Yerushalayim (may it be rebuilt and established) and is connected to the building project for the Chief Rabbinate, visited here, he spoke about this and expressed his willingness to assist the Chabad Gemach funds to the best of his ability. Therefore, it is good that you contact him in an appropriate manner. Certainly, you should also inform us here regarding the steps you have taken in all of the above; the sooner this is done, and the more detailed the correspondence to us, the better.

With respect and blessing.

It is understood that it is most correct and good that before acting on the above, all the anash Gemach funds should consult among themselves regarding a plan of action and an appropriate format.

## 5,285

5,285

B”H, 17 Adar II, 5717
Brooklyn.

To the esteemed Chossid, G-d-fearing and learned, etc., R’ Dovid Meir, may he live.

Shalom u’Bracha!

I hereby acknowledge receipt of your *pan* (pidyon nefesh), which will be read at an auspicious time at the holy resting place of my father-in-law, the Rebbe, of blessed memory.

It is regrettable that you did not participate in the *farbrengen* of several tens of *anash* and descendants of *anash*, for where ten are gathered, the *Shechinah* rests—and all the more so when there are several tens. Even though the *halachah* regarding *Birchas Hamazon* states that there is no difference in the text between ten and one hundred, the very possibility of this dispute—especially the opinion of those who argue that both are "words of the living G-d"—proves that as the number and quantity increase, the quality increases as well. In truth, this is also proven in the *nigleh* (the revealed Torah), for in many *mitzvos* and matters, the *halachah* is decided in accordance with the principle that "in the multitude of people is the King’s glory," including the laws regarding the reading of the *Megillah*, from which we derive this principle*. And although you wrote that you were forced by circumstances to be absent, we do not say "as if he had performed it" regarding one who is exempt by the Merciful One.

May it be Hashem’s will that, at the very least, in the future you will be able to fulfill your heart’s desires for the good by participating amidst a large gathering in matters of Torah and *mitzvos* in general, and matters of *Chassidus* in particular. May all *he'lem* (concealments) and *hesterim* (hiddenness) be nullified, and not only will they not hinder you, but they will actually assist you. This is the unique quality of the month of Purim, regarding which our holy Torah says: "And the month [i.e., all the days of the month] which was turned for them..." into joy and gladness.

The concept of "turned" (*nehepach*) means that even those matters which do not appear good in a revealed and manifest way, this month has the power to transform into joy and gladness that are visible to physical eyes.

With blessings,

## 587

B"H, 17 Adar Sheni, 5717
Brooklyn.

Greetings and Blessings!

...Regarding the health of your father, shlita — I will mention him at the Ohel for a great improvement in this regard. However, in general, the matter depends primarily on him himself—or more accurately, on his avodah in the yeshivah... that it should be as it ought to be in truth, for then he will be truly healthy and strong. And "as it ought to be" means with the greatest possible interest, without sparing time or energy. And the exertion, etc., etc. — at the very least, to the same degree that one concerns oneself with the healing of the body and its health. And the intention here is not to argue with me, but rather that it should be directed toward the Healer of all flesh, and the intelligent will understand...

And regarding what you wrote about sadness, etc. — the matter has already been addressed in the Tanya Kadisha, that the source of sadness is impure. And what does he have to do with such a thing? I am astonished: where do anash find free time for such matters? Do they not see and feel that time nowadays is more precious than anything, and that every moment is a matter that must be utilized for its purpose? And yet, how many of them waste their time on matters that cannot even be called "trivial"!! Perhaps this is also the explanation for the double concealment, "And I will surely conceal (haster astir) My face on that day," and may it be His will that very soon it will be fulfilled, "And it will be said on that day, 'Behold, this is our G-d... this' [is the one we hoped for]—doubled."

#### ***587***

A portion was printed in Likkutei Sichos, Vol. 14, p. 356, and it was completed based on the secretariat’s copy.

## 5288

B"H, 17 Adar Sheni, 5717
Brooklyn.

To the esteemed, G-d-fearing, and distinguished individual, energetic in the needs of the community,
R' Avraham Chanoch, may he live.

Peace and blessing!

I hereby acknowledge receipt of your letters from 26 Teves, 27 Shevat, 12 and 23 Adar Rishon, and 11 Adar Sheni. You should certainly continue writing in the future with greater detail. It is surprising that for a long time now, no clippings from the newspapers have been received, even though they surely printed items regarding Chabad matters during this time. And if you mean to say that they did not print anything, then the wonder is even greater.

The sichos translated up to page 81 have been received, though time has not yet permitted me to review them as required.

In your letter of 23 Adar Rishon, you write that you would be visiting Mr. A. S. in those days, and since then you have not mentioned anything further regarding this. Surely you will also provide an update on this in your next letter, as well as whether you met with those you intended to speak with regarding Mr. Friedberg’s book.

Awaiting good news regarding all the above,

The books of the esteemed Rabbi, etc., R' Margalit, may he live, are in the library: Zohar, Tikkunei Zohar, Zohar Chadash, and Sefer HaBahir with its annotations (all of these are the first editions). Shaarei Zohar, the Rambam booklet, and Sefer HaZohar. It is understood that we are interested in subsequent editions if they contain additions. It is appropriate to give the aforementioned individual the Sdei Chemed as a gift, with the emphasis that this is the custom for those who send all of their books to the library here.
